allOutcomes.title: CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke, or HF Hospitalization
Kidney Failure, ≥40% eGFR Decline, or Renal Death

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:33264825 (Bakris GL et al. NEJM 2020;383:2219-2239); ClinicalTrials.gov NCT02540993
evidence.text: A total of 5,734 patients were screened; 5,674 underwent randomization, with 2,833 assigned to finerenone and 2,841 assigned to placebo. [Source: PubMed abstract PMID:33264825 — "5734 patients...2833 assigned to finerenone and 2841 assigned to placebo."]

evidence.label: Key Secondary CV Composite Outcome
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:33264825 (Bakris GL et al. NEJM 2020;383:2219-2239); CT.gov NCT02540993 results API v2
evidence.text: The key secondary outcome (a composite of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, nonfatal stroke, or hospitalization for heart failure) occurred in 367 of 2,833 patients (13.0%) in the finerenone group and 420 of 2,841 patients (14.8%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.75 to 0.99; P=0.03). [Source: PubMed abstract states "367 patients (13.0%) and 420 patients (14.8%)...hazard ratio, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.75 to 0.99; P=0.03"; CT.gov results confirm 367/2833 vs 420/2841, HR 0.860 (0.747-0.989), P=0.0339]

evidence.label: Primary Renal Composite Outcome
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:33264825 (Bakris GL et al. NEJM 2020;383:2219-2239); CT.gov NCT02540993 results API v2
evidence.text: The primary outcome (a composite of kidney failure, a sustained decrease of at least 40% in the eGFR from baseline, or death from renal causes) occurred in 504 of 2,833 patients (17.8%) in the finerenone group and 600 of 2,841 patients (21.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.82; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.93; P=0.001). [Source: PubMed abstract states "504 of 2833 patients (17.8%)...600 of 2841 patients (21.1%)...hazard ratio, 0.82; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.93; P=0.001"; CT.gov results confirm 504/2833 vs 600/2841, HR 0.825 (0.732-0.928), P=0.0014]

evidence.label: All-Cause Mortality
evidence.source: CT.gov NCT02540993 results API v2 (secondary outcome measure); cross-validated with WikiJournalClub FIDELIO-DKD summary
evidence.text: Death from any cause occurred in 219 of 2,833 patients (7.7%) in the finerenone group and 244 of 2,841 patients (8.6%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.895; 95% CI, 0.746 to 1.075; P=0.2348; not statistically significant). [Source: CT.gov results section — registered secondary outcome "Time to All-cause Mortality": 219/2833 vs 244/2841, HR 0.895 (0.746-1.075), P=0.2348]

evidence.label: Hospitalization for Heart Failure
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:33264825 (component of CV composite); CT.gov NCT02540993 (denominator); Filippatos et al. Circulation 2021 PMID:33198491 (HR derivation)
evidence.text: Hospitalization for heart failure occurred in 139 of 2,833 patients (4.9%) in the finerenone group and 162 of 2,841 patients (5.7%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.68 to 1.08; not statistically significant). [Source: HF hospitalization is a component of the CV composite. Event counts (139 and 162) from Filippatos et al. Circulation 2021 PMID:33198491; percentages confirmed by back-calculation from CT.gov denominators (139/2833=4.91%, 162/2841=5.70%, consistent with rounded 4.9%/5.7%). HR 0.86 (0.68-1.08) from PMID:33198491 forest plot data]

evidence.label: Hyperkalemia (Safety Outcome)
evidence.source: FDA Clinical Review NDA 215341 (FIDELIO-DKD); Rossing P et al. JASN 2022;33:1507-1520 PMID:35589390
evidence.text: Hyperkalemia (serum potassium ≥5.5 mmol/L, MedDRA preferred term) occurred in 395 of 2,827 patients (14.0%) in the finerenone group and 194 of 2,831 patients (6.9%) in the placebo group (safety analysis set). Denominators reflect the safety population (patients who received at least one dose of study drug), which differs from the full analysis set (2,833/2,841). [Source: FDA Clinical Review Table 26 reports exact event counts; Rossing et al. JASN 2022 PMID:35589390 confirms 14.0% vs 6.9% incidence]

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.source: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT02540993 protocol; PubMed abstract PMID:33264825
evidence.text: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, event-driven phase III trial. Independent data monitoring committee with pre-specified stopping rules. Centralized randomization via IVRS with stratification by region, eGFR, and UACR. Blinding maintained through matching placebo. D3 (Missing data): Low — event-driven design with pre-specified analysis population (full analysis set), 99.8% vital status ascertained. D5 (Selective reporting): Low — pre-registered on CT.gov (NCT02540993) before enrollment; all primary and key secondary outcomes reported as registered. [Source: CT.gov protocol and PubMed abstract confirm design details]

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Men or women ≥18 years of age~* Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2D) as defined by the American Diabetes Association~* Diagnosis of chronic kidney disease (CKD) with at least one of the following criteria at run-in and screening visits:~  * persistent high albuminuria (UACR ≥30 to <300 mg/g in 2 out of 3 first morning void samples) and estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) ≥25 but <60 mL/min/1.73 m² (CKD EPI) and presence of diabetic retinopathy or~ * persistent very hig...

allOutcomes.title: CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke, or HF Hospitalization
Kidney Failure, ≥40% eGFR Decline, or Renal Death

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 (Pitt B et al. NEJM 2021;385:2252-2263); ClinicalTrials.gov NCT02545049
evidence.text: A total of 7,437 patients were randomly assigned: 3,686 to receive finerenone and 3,666 to receive placebo, with 85 subsequently excluded from the full analysis set. [Source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 — "7437 patients...3686 to finerenone and 3666 to placebo"]

evidence.label: Primary CV Composite Outcome
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 (Pitt B et al. NEJM 2021;385:2252-2263); CT.gov NCT02545049 results API v2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event (a composite of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, nonfatal stroke, or hospitalization for heart failure) occurred in 458 of 3,686 patients (12.4%) in the finerenone group and in 519 of 3,666 patients (14.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.76 to 0.98; P=0.03). [Source: PubMed abstract states "458 of 3686 patients (12.4%)...519 of 3666 (14.2%)...hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.76 to 0.98"; CT.gov results confirm 458/3686 vs 519/3666, HR 0.87 (0.76-0.98), P=0.0264]

evidence.label: Renal Composite Outcome
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 (Pitt B et al. NEJM 2021;385:2252-2263); CT.gov NCT02545049 results API v2
evidence.text: The first key secondary outcome (a composite of kidney failure, a sustained decrease from baseline of at least 40% in the eGFR, or death from renal causes) occurred in 350 of 3,686 patients (9.5%) in the finerenone group and 395 of 3,666 patients (10.8%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.76 to 1.01; not statistically significant). [Source: PubMed abstract states "350 patients (9.5%)...395 (10.8%)...hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.76 to 1.01"; CT.gov results confirm 350/3686 vs 395/3666, HR 0.87 (0.76-1.01), P=0.0689]

evidence.label: All-Cause Mortality
evidence.source: CT.gov NCT02545049 results API v2 (registered secondary outcome)
evidence.text: Death from any cause occurred in 333 of 3,686 patients (9.0%) in the finerenone group and 370 of 3,666 patients (10.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.89; 95% CI, 0.77 to 1.04; P=0.1337; not statistically significant). [Source: CT.gov results section — registered secondary outcome "Time to All-cause Mortality": 333/3686 vs 370/3666, HR 0.89 (0.77-1.04), P=0.1337]

evidence.label: Hospitalization for Heart Failure
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 (HR from primary paper abstract); PubMed abstract PMID:34775784 (Filippatos et al. Circulation 2022, HF sub-study); CT.gov NCT02545049 (denominators)
evidence.text: Hospitalization for heart failure occurred in 117 of 3,686 patients (3.2%) in the finerenone group and 163 of 3,666 patients (4.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.56 to 0.90; P=0.004). This component drove much of the benefit observed in the primary CV composite. [Source: PubMed abstract PMID:34449181 reports "hazard ratio, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.56 to 0.90" for HF hospitalization. Event counts (117 and 163) from HF sub-study PMID:34775784 (Filippatos et al. Circulation 2022); percentages 3.2% and 4.4% confirmed by back-calculation from CT.gov denominators (117/3686=3.17%, 163/3666=4.45%, consistent with rounded 3.2%/4.4%)]

evidence.label: Hyperkalemia (Safety Outcome)
evidence.source: FDA Clinical Review NDA 215341 (pooled FIDELITY analysis); derived by subtraction from FIDELIO counts
evidence.text: Hyperkalemia (serum potassium ≥5.5 mmol/L, MedDRA preferred term) occurred in 517 of 3,683 patients (14.0%) in the finerenone group and 254 of 3,658 patients (6.9%) in the placebo group (safety analysis set). FIGARO counts derived from the FDA pooled FIDELITY totals (912/6,510 finerenone vs 448/6,489 placebo) minus FIDELIO counts (395/2,827 vs 194/2,831). [Source: FDA NDA 215341 pooled safety tables; back-calculation: 912-395=517, 6510-2827=3683, 448-194=254, 6489-2831=3658]

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.source: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT02545049 protocol; PubMed abstract PMID:34449181
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter, event-driven phase III trial. Central randomization stratified by region, eGFR category, and albuminuria level. Independent DSMB with charter-defined interim analyses. D3 (Missing data): Low — event-driven design with full analysis set; vital status ascertained for >99% of participants. D5 (Selective reporting): Low — pre-registered on CT.gov (NCT02545049) before enrollment; all primary and key secondary outcomes reported per protocol. [Source: CT.gov protocol and PubMed abstract confirm design details]

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Men or women ≥18 years of age~* Subjects with Type Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus as defined by the American Diabetes Association~* Diagnosis of Diabetic Kidney Disease with persistent high albuminuria or persistent very high albuminuria at the Run-In and Screening Visit~* Pretreated with either angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor (ACEI) or angiotensin receptor blocker (ARB) at maximal tolerated labeled dose without adjustments~* Serum potassium <=4.8 mmol/L~Exclusion Crite...

allOutcomes.title: First Worsening HF Event or CV Death

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:39225278 (Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2024;391:1475-1485); ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04435626
evidence.text: A total of 6,001 patients with heart failure and a left ventricular ejection fraction of 40% or more were enrolled; 3,003 were assigned to finerenone and 2,998 to placebo. [Source: PubMed abstract PMID:39225278 — "6001 patients...3003 to finerenone and 2998 to placebo"; CT.gov NCT04435626 confirms enrollment of 6,016]

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:39225278 (Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2024;391:1475-1485); CT.gov NCT04435626 results API v2
evidence.text: Time-to-first worsening heart failure event or cardiovascular death occurred in 624 of 3,003 patients (20.8%) in the finerenone group and 719 of 2,998 patients (24.0%) in the placebo group (rate ratio 0.84, 95% CI 0.74 to 0.95; P=0.007). In the recurrent-event primary framework, total worsening heart failure events plus cardiovascular death were 1,083 vs 1,283. [Source: PubMed abstract states "1083 primary-outcome events...in 624 of 3003 patients...1283...in 719 of 2998 patients (rate ratio, 0.84; 95% CI, 0.74 to 0.95; P=0.007)"; CT.gov results confirm 1083 vs 1283 events, 624 vs 719 participants, RR 0.84 (0.74-0.95), P=0.0072]

evidence.label: All-Cause Mortality
evidence.source: CT.gov NCT04435626 results API v2 (registered secondary outcome)
evidence.text: Death from any cause occurred in 491 of 3,003 patients (16.4%) in the finerenone group and 522 of 2,998 patients (17.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.93; 95% CI, 0.83 to 1.06; P=0.2794; not statistically significant). [Source: CT.gov results section — registered secondary outcome "All-cause Mortality": 491/3003 vs 522/2998, HR 0.93 (0.83-1.06), P=0.2794]

evidence.label: Kidney Composite Outcome
evidence.source: CT.gov NCT04435626 results API v2 (registered secondary outcome); PubMed abstract PMID:39490700 (Mc Causland FR et al. JACC 2024, kidney sub-study)
evidence.text: The kidney composite outcome (sustained eGFR decrease of 50% or more, sustained eGFR below 15 ml/min/1.73 m2, initiation of long-term dialysis, or kidney transplantation) occurred in 75 of 3,003 patients (2.5%) in the finerenone group and 55 of 2,998 patients (1.8%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 1.33; 95% CI, 0.94 to 1.89; P=0.1071; not statistically significant). Note: numerically higher in the finerenone group (harm direction), but not significant; likely reflects the initial hemodynamic dip in eGFR seen with MRA initiation. [Source: CT.gov results confirm 75/3003 vs 55/2998, HR 1.33 (0.94-1.89), P=0.1071. PubMed abstract PMID:39490700 confirms "75 vs 55 events; HR: 1.33; 95% CI: 0.94-1.89"]

evidence.label: Hyperkalemia (Safety Outcome)
evidence.source: FDA label for finerenone (Kerendia); Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2024 PMID:39225278 safety table
evidence.text: Hyperkalemia (serum potassium ≥5.5 mmol/L) occurred in approximately 290 of 2,993 patients (9.7%) in the finerenone group and 126 of 2,996 patients (4.2%) in the placebo group (safety analysis set). Note: event counts derived from FDA label percentages (9.7% and 4.2%) applied to safety population denominators; these are approximate counts. [Source: FDA Kerendia label Section 6.1 reports 9.7% vs 4.2% hyperkalemia incidence in FINEARTS-HF; denominators from safety analysis set]

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.source: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04435626 protocol; PubMed abstract PMID:39225278
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, event-driven, multicenter phase III trial in HFmrEF/HFpEF. Block randomization via IVRS. Adjudication of all primary outcome events by an independent clinical events committee blinded to treatment assignment. D3 (Missing data): Low — full analysis set with <1% lost to follow-up; event-driven design. D5 (Selective reporting): Low — pre-registered on CT.gov (NCT04435626) before enrollment; all primary and key secondary outcomes reported per protocol. [Source: CT.gov protocol and PubMed abstract confirm design details]

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Participant (male or female) must be aged 40 years and older.~* Diagnosis of heart failure with New York Heart Association(NYHA) class II-IV, ambulatory or hospitalized primarily for heart failure.~* On diuretic treatment for at least 30 days prior to randomization.~* Documented left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) of ≥40% measured by any modality within the last 12 months.~* Structural heart abnormalities based on any local imaging measurement within the last 12 mont...

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:26325557 (Bakris GL et al. JAMA 2015;314:884-894); ClinicalTrials.gov NCT01874431
evidence.text: Of 1,501 patients screened, 821 were included in the analysis: 727 randomized to finerenone (pooled across 1.25mg, 2.5mg, 5mg, 7.5mg, 10mg, 15mg, 25mg dose groups) and 94 to placebo. Note: Dose-finding design with highly imbalanced randomization (7 active arms vs 1 placebo). [Source: CT.gov NCT01874431 confirms enrollment 823, Phase 2; PubMed abstract PMID:26325557 confirms dose arms and randomization]

evidence.label: Cardiovascular Events
evidence.source: PubMed abstract PMID:26325557 (Bakris GL et al. JAMA 2015;314:884-894); ClinicalTrials.gov NCT01874431 results
evidence.text: Cardiovascular events occurred in 3 of 727 patients (0.4%) in the pooled finerenone group and 1 of 94 patients (1.1%) in the placebo group during 90-day follow-up. Note: Phase IIb dose-finding design with short follow-up; not powered for clinical events. Primary endpoint was UACR change. [Source: CT.gov NCT01874431 has results posted; event counts cross-validated with Li et al. Front Endocrinol 2024 meta-analysis]

evidence.label: RoB Assessment — D3: Missing Data
evidence.source: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT01874431 protocol and results; PubMed abstract PMID:26325557
evidence.text: Some concerns: differential discontinuation across dose groups (range 5.6%–12.3% in finerenone arms vs 8.0% in placebo), inherent to dose-finding design. Highly imbalanced randomization (7:1) limits comparability. 90-day follow-up is insufficient for reliable CV event ascertainment. [Source: Discontinuation rates from CT.gov results and PubMed abstract]

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Men and women aged 18 years and older.The lower age limit may be higher if legally required in the participating country~* Women of childbearing potential can only be included in the study if a pregnancy test is negative and if they agree to use adequate contraception when sexually active.~* Subjects with type 2 diabetes mellitus fulfilling at least 1 of the following criteria~  * are on oral antidiabetics and / or insulin,~ * have a documented fasting glucose >/= 7.0 mmo...
